title: daucus kotovii
intro: Daucus carota is sometimes referred to as Daucus kotovii, however, it is actually Daucus carota, and is a species of wild carrot native to Europe and Western Asia. It belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is a biennial plant with a single stem that grows up to 3 feet high. The medicinal properties of Daucus carota include its high content of vitamins A and K, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Daucus carota include supporting eye health, immune function, and skin health, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures.
